title: Homepage TUI E2E evidence, Snake walkthrough playback, and inline file viewer default

context.problem_summary: The homepage TUI recording must be a real end-to-end evidence artifact, not a fake or renderer-only placeholder. It must be produced by an actual ananta tui run, include visible Snake interaction, show navigation toward Artifacts, start the Tutorial/AI conversation through a local LM Studio/OpenAI-compatible backend, and play back correctly on the website. In parallel, normal TUI file opening should default to an inline read-only file viewer instead of immediately exec-ing an external editor such as vim.
observed_current_state: scripts/e2e/record_tui_demo.py now contains a snake-mode-live-e2e scene that starts an actual process through PTY and scripts navigation/interaction.
scripts/e2e/record_tui_demo.py writes Asciinema cast metadata using actual width/height values after the recent fix.
The earlier snake-mode-live scene renders TUI frames through render_operator_shell, but is still renderer-generated evidence, not a real PTY execution.
agent/services/editor_resolver.py still resolves --open actions toward configured external editor tools and ultimately vim fallback.
agent/cli/commands/tui_editor.py still calls exec_fn(argv), and default exec uses os.execvp, meaning the TUI process is replaced by an external editor instead of opening an inline viewer.
context.target_outcome: A deterministic, testable, homepage-ready E2E flow where the displayed cast is traceably generated from a real TUI run and where file open actions use an inline viewer by default, while external editors remain explicit opt-in tools.

in_scope: Real PTY-based homepage TUI recording flow
LM Studio/OpenAI-compatible Tutorial AI integration for the recording
Deterministic scripted Snake interaction and Artifacts navigation
Asciinema cast validation and web playback validation
Inline read-only file viewer as default for TUI file opening
Tests proving no fake/static cast is used for homepage evidence
Documentation for local reproduction and CI-safe fallbacks
out_of_scope: Cloud LLM requirement for homepage evidence
Making LM Studio mandatory in normal CI
Large UI redesign beyond the minimum needed for inline viewer and cast evidence

design_principles: Evidence first: homepage cast must be generated from the same command path a user can run locally.
No silent fake: renderer-only or prebuilt placeholders may be used only as explicit fallback artifacts, never labeled as live E2E evidence.
Deterministic defaults: file open behavior must be predictable and testable.
Least privilege: inline viewer is read-only by default; write-capable external editors require explicit user action or policy.
No shell expansion for file paths: file operations and external tools must use validated paths and argv arrays.
Graceful degradation: CI may skip LM Studio-dependent live evidence, but must still validate the flow contract and mark skipped evidence honestly.

implementation_plan.title: Define homepage evidence artifact contract
implementation_plan.description: Define exactly what qualifies as a homepage TUI evidence cast and how fake/static/renderer-only variants are labeled.
tasks: Create a small contract document under docs/testing/homepage_tui_e2e_contract.md.
Document required scene name: snake-mode-live-e2e.
Document required artifact outputs: tests/output/operator_tui_splash.cast and web/www/assets/operator_tui_splash.cast.
Document required proof markers inside cast frames: ananta tui startup, Snake mode active, Artifacts navigation, Tutorial AI enabled, user question, AI/backend response, quit/clean shutdown.
Document which fallbacks are allowed and how they must be named.
acceptance_criteria: A reviewer can tell from documentation whether a cast is real PTY E2E, renderer-generated demo, or placeholder fallback.
The homepage asset operator_tui_splash.cast is not ambiguously described as live evidence unless it came from snake-mode-live-e2e.
The contract explicitly states that renderer-only snake-mode-live is not sufficient for homepage E2E evidence.
The contract includes a minimal local command for reproducing the evidence with LM Studio.
The contract includes a CI-safe path that skips real LM Studio dependency without pretending success.
suggested_tests: Markdown lint or simple docs existence test if docs tests exist.

implementation_plan.title: Add evidence metadata sidecar for generated casts
implementation_plan.description: Every generated homepage cast should have machine-readable metadata proving how it was generated.
tasks: Extend scripts/e2e/record_tui_demo.py to write a sidecar JSON for snake-mode-live-e2e runs.
Include scene, run_id, command, width, height, duration_limit, started_at, finished_at, exit_code, backend type, model, API base without token, sync targets, and proof marker summary.
Write sidecar next to the cast in the artifact root.
When syncing to web/www/assets/operator_tui_splash.cast, also sync or generate web/www/assets/operator_tui_splash.meta.json.
Never store API tokens or secrets in the metadata.
acceptance_criteria: Running python3 scripts/e2e/record_tui_demo.py --enable --scene snake-mode-live-e2e creates a .cast and a .meta.json file.
The metadata contains scene=snake-mode-live-e2e and source=pty-process or equivalent explicit marker.
The metadata contains the exact terminal dimensions that match the Asciinema header.
The metadata does not contain ANANTA_TUI_LLM_API_TOKEN or any bearer token-like value.
The synced website metadata exists beside web/www/assets/operator_tui_splash.cast.
A test fails if the metadata claims live E2E but the command was not executed through the PTY capture path.

implementation_plan.title: Strengthen real PTY capture validation
implementation_plan.description: The current test monkeypatches the live cast function and mostly verifies dispatch. Add tests that validate the real PTY capture path contract without requiring LM Studio in normal CI.
tasks: Add an injectable fake TUI command script that emits deterministic prompt_toolkit-like frames and reacts to scripted key input.
Run snake-mode-live-e2e with ANANTA_TUI_E2E_CAST_COMMAND pointing to the fake command in tests.
Assert PTY output is captured from subprocess stdout/stderr, not generated by render_operator_shell directly.
Assert scripted key events are sent in expected order.
Assert timeout sends q and terminates cleanly.
acceptance_criteria: A unit/integration test exercises _snake_mode_live_e2e_cast without monkeypatching the function itself.
The test proves the command from ANANTA_TUI_E2E_CAST_COMMAND is actually launched.
The test proves at least one scripted input reaches the child process.
The test fails if _snake_mode_live_e2e_cast is replaced by static text generation.
The test is stable on Linux CI and does not require LM Studio.
The real LM Studio run remains available as an opt-in local evidence run.

implementation_plan.title: Add proof markers to the real TUI walkthrough
implementation_plan.description: Make the actual homepage recording self-verifying by ensuring visible and parseable markers appear in frames.
tasks: Ensure the live TUI displays a stable status marker for Snake mode active.
Ensure Artifacts navigation target is visibly selected or named in the frame text.
Ensure Tutorial AI enabled state is visibly displayed.
Ensure the user questions are visible in history/status/chat area.
Ensure the AI/backend response source is visible as local/openai-compatible/lmstudio without leaking secrets.
Add a small cast parser helper that strips ANSI escapes and searches for proof markers.
acceptance_criteria: The generated cast contains a visible Snake-mode marker after Ctrl+S is sent.
The generated cast contains the word Artifacts or a stable section id for artifacts navigation.
The generated cast contains a visible Tutorial AI enabled/on marker.
The generated cast contains at least one user question from the scripted walkthrough.
The generated cast contains at least one AI/backend response marker.
A test parses the cast text and asserts all markers are present after stripping ANSI escape sequences.
The marker test fails against the old placeholder cast.

implementation_plan.title: Validate web playback uses current homepage cast asset
implementation_plan.description: The website must load and play the generated operator_tui_splash.cast, not an old fallback or broken path.
tasks: Locate the website code path that references operator_tui_splash.cast.
Add a web asset test that checks the referenced cast exists under web/www/assets/.
Add a test that parses the Asciinema header and asserts version=2, width>=80, height>=20, and title contains Snake Mode Live E2E or an explicitly accepted title.
Add a test that ensures the web asset and tests/output artifact are byte-identical when sync is requested.
If website supports fallback casts, make fallback label visible in HTML or metadata.
acceptance_criteria: web/www/assets/operator_tui_splash.cast exists after the evidence run.
The homepage references the same path that the evidence sync writes.
The web asset parses as valid Asciinema v2 JSON-lines.
The web asset contains terminal dimensions matching the generated run, not stale hardcoded values.
The web asset is not silently replaced by a legacy splash cast during tests.
A test fails if the website path points to a missing or stale file.

implementation_plan.title: Separate renderer demo scene from live E2E scene in naming and outputs
implementation_plan.description: Avoid confusing renderer-generated snake-mode-live with true PTY-based snake-mode-live-e2e.
tasks: Rename or clearly document renderer-only snake-mode-live as renderer-demo if backwards compatibility allows.
Keep snake-mode-live-e2e as the only scene allowed to sync to homepage live evidence by default.
If old scene names must remain, add explicit metadata source=renderer-demo.
Update docs/testing/e2e_artifact_review.md and docs/testing/e2e_visual_evidence.md accordingly.
Add test preventing renderer-only scene from overwriting homepage E2E cast unless explicitly forced.
acceptance_criteria: A developer cannot accidentally generate homepage live evidence using renderer-only scene with default commands.
Renderer demo artifacts are named differently or clearly marked in metadata.
The docs explain the difference in one short table.
A test fails if scene=snake-mode-live writes source=pty-process metadata.
A test fails if scene=snake-mode-live-e2e writes source=renderer-demo metadata.

implementation_plan.title: Introduce inline read-only file viewer model
implementation_plan.description: Add a first-class inline viewer mode that can render file contents inside the Ananta TUI without replacing the TUI process.
tasks: Add an InlineFileViewerState or equivalent model under client_surfaces/operator_tui/models.py or a dedicated viewer module.
Track file path, display path, language/file type, readonly flag, current scroll offset, selected line, total lines, truncated flag, binary/too-large flag, and error state.
Add renderer support for a central viewer panel showing line numbers and file content.
Add footer/help hints for Up/Down/PageUp/PageDown/Home/End/q/back/open-external.
Handle empty files, missing files, binary files, and large files gracefully.
acceptance_criteria: A text file can be displayed inside the TUI without calling os.execvp.
The viewer shows at least path, readonly marker, line numbers, and content area.
Scrolling works deterministically and does not crash at file boundaries.
Large files are capped or windowed with a visible truncated/partial marker.
Binary or invalid UTF-8 files show a clear non-text warning instead of dumping unreadable bytes.
The viewer state can be rendered through existing TUI renderer tests without requiring a terminal subprocess.

implementation_plan.title: Make inline viewer the default for ananta tui --open
implementation_plan.description: Change default open behavior so --open displays an inline read-only viewer unless the user explicitly asks for an external editor.
tasks: Extend agent/cli/commands/tui_editor.py with an explicit mode argument such as --external-editor or --edit.
Keep --with EDITOR as explicit external editor selection, not default viewer behavior.
Change --open FILE with no external flag to start or render inline viewer mode.
Preserve workspace path validation before opening the file.
Ensure readonly is true by default for inline viewing.
Return non-zero for invalid paths without launching any external tool.
acceptance_criteria: ananta tui --open README.md does not call os.execvp by default.
ananta tui --open README.md renders or starts inline viewer mode.
ananta tui --open README.md --with vim or --external-editor vim still launches the external editor if allowed.
The old external editor path remains covered by tests but is explicit opt-in.
Path traversal attempts such as ../secret fail before viewer/editor launch.
The command help clearly explains default inline viewer versus explicit external editor.

implementation_plan.title: Split editor resolution from viewer resolution
implementation_plan.description: The current EditorResolver handles external editor selection. Add a separate deterministic open-action resolver that decides viewer vs external editor.
tasks: Introduce an OpenActionResolver or FileOpenResolver service.
Resolution result should include mode=inline_viewer or mode=external_editor.
Default mode must be inline_viewer for normal --open.
External editor resolution should delegate to existing EditorResolver only after explicit opt-in.
Add reason codes such as default_inline_viewer, explicit_external_editor, explicit_with_editor, policy_forced_readonly_viewer.
Keep EditorResolver behavior intact for external editor mode to avoid breaking existing tests.
acceptance_criteria: A resolver test proves default file open resolves to inline_viewer with reason default_inline_viewer.
A resolver test proves --with vim resolves to external_editor with reason explicit_with_editor.
A resolver test proves project/user filetype editor rules do not override default inline viewer unless external mode is requested.
A resolver test proves policy can force inline readonly viewer even when external editor is requested.
Existing EditorResolver tests continue to pass for external editor mode.

implementation_plan.title: Add inline viewer event handling and navigation
implementation_plan.description: The inline viewer needs basic interactive navigation so it is useful as a practical default.
tasks: Wire Up/Down to single-line scroll.
Wire PageUp/PageDown to viewport-sized scroll.
Wire Home/End to top/bottom.
Wire q/Esc/back to return to previous TUI pane.
Wire / or search only if a minimal deterministic search implementation already fits; otherwise defer and document.
Keep key handling separate from external editor handling.
acceptance_criteria: Scrolling down changes first visible line by one for Down and by viewport size for PageDown.
Scrolling never produces negative offsets or offsets beyond last line.
Home always jumps to line 1.
End jumps so the last line is visible.
q or Esc exits viewer mode and restores previous pane/state.
Viewer key handling tests do not require a real terminal.

implementation_plan.title: Connect Artifacts selection to inline viewer
implementation_plan.description: The homepage walkthrough navigates to Artifacts, so selected artifacts should open through the inline viewer by default.
tasks: Identify the Artifacts pane/section data model in the operator TUI.
Add an open-selected-artifact action that validates the artifact path.
Route artifact open action to inline viewer by default.
Add explicit external editor action only as separate key or command.
Update status/help text to mention inline viewer behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Selecting a text artifact and pressing the documented open key opens it in the inline viewer.
Selecting a missing artifact shows a TUI error state and does not crash.
Selecting a binary artifact shows binary warning in viewer state.
No external editor is launched by artifact open default.
The homepage E2E cast can visibly navigate to Artifacts and open or preview a relevant artifact inline.

implementation_plan.title: Policy and permissions for viewer vs editor
implementation_plan.description: Opening a read-only inline viewer should be lower-risk than launching a write-capable editor. Encode that distinction explicitly.
tasks: Define permissions tui.file.view, tui.editor.external.launch, tui.editor.write if permission model exists.
Default local user may view files within workspace.
External editor launch requires explicit permission or local-only policy.
Write-capable editor path must never be implied by readonly viewer action.
Document hard denies for outside-workspace paths and model-supplied editor commands.
acceptance_criteria: Permission checks distinguish read-only inline viewer from external editor launch.
A denied external editor launch can still fall back to inline readonly viewer if viewing is allowed.
A denied view action does not fall back to external editor.
No LLM/model response can directly provide arbitrary external editor command without policy approval.
Tests cover allowed viewer, denied viewer, allowed external editor, denied external editor.

implementation_plan.title: Update user-facing TUI open/edit documentation
implementation_plan.description: Docs must stop implying that default file open means external editor takeover.
tasks: Update docs/editor-tui-foundation.md with inline viewer as default behavior.
Update CLI examples for ananta tui --open README.md.
Add examples for explicit external editor opt-in.
Add troubleshooting for LM Studio homepage evidence run.
Add note that web homepage evidence should be regenerated from snake-mode-live-e2e.
acceptance_criteria: Docs show default: ananta tui --open README.md opens inline read-only viewer.
Docs show explicit external: ananta tui --open README.md --with vim or selected final syntax.
Docs mention that external editor can replace/own terminal session and is not the default.
Docs include exact LM Studio env vars for evidence recording.
Docs clearly mark CI skip behavior for missing LM Studio.

implementation_plan.title: Add CI-safe evidence validation lane
implementation_plan.description: CI should validate contracts and fake PTY path, while the real LM Studio path remains opt-in/local.
tasks: Add a CI-safe test marker for PTY fake command tests.
Add optional marker for live LM Studio tests, skipped unless ANANTA_TUI_LLM_API_BASE is reachable and ANANTA_ENABLE_LIVE_LMSTUDIO_E2E=1.
Make skip reason explicit in test output.
Do not update committed homepage cast from CI unless explicitly requested.
Add a Makefile/script shortcut if the repo uses such conventions.
acceptance_criteria: Default CI validates fake PTY capture and cast parser/metadata tests.
Default CI does not fail solely because LM Studio is not running.
Opt-in local live test fails if LM Studio endpoint is configured but unreachable.
Opt-in local live test produces cast + metadata + proof marker validation.
CI logs clearly distinguish skipped live evidence from passed mocked/fake PTY contract tests.

implementation_plan.title: Add final regression checklist for homepage TUI evidence
implementation_plan.description: Create a short repeatable checklist to prevent future regressions back to fake/static homepage cast evidence.
tasks: Add docs/testing/homepage_tui_e2e_checklist.md.
Checklist should include command to generate cast, command to validate cast, command to inspect metadata, and command to serve/check website playback.
Include manual screenshot/video review points: Snake visible, Artifacts selected, Tutorial AI response visible, inline viewer visible if implemented in walkthrough.
Include failure triage for stale cast, wrong dimensions, missing LM Studio, ANSI rendering issue, and path mismatch.
acceptance_criteria: A developer can regenerate and validate the homepage TUI cast by following only the checklist.
The checklist contains exact commands, not vague prose only.
The checklist includes expected output files.
The checklist includes at least five common failure modes with direct next steps.
The checklist references this todo id for traceability.
suggested_tests: Optional docs existence/link test if docs test framework exists.

global_acceptance_criteria: Homepage TUI cast can be generated by a documented real ananta tui PTY run.
Generated homepage cast contains visible Snake interaction, Artifacts navigation, Tutorial AI activation, user question, and AI/backend response markers.
Asciinema header dimensions match the actual configured run dimensions.
web/www/assets/operator_tui_splash.cast is valid Asciinema v2 JSON-lines and is the asset referenced by the homepage.
A sidecar metadata file exists and proves whether the artifact is PTY E2E, renderer demo, or placeholder fallback.
Renderer-only demo scenes cannot silently overwrite homepage E2E evidence as if they were live evidence.
ananta tui --open FILE defaults to inline read-only viewer and does not exec an external editor.
External editor launching remains available only through explicit opt-in and policy validation.
Workspace path validation protects both inline viewer and external editor paths.
All new tests pass without requiring LM Studio by default, while optional LM Studio evidence tests are available and honestly skipped when not enabled.
risk_notes: Changing --open default behavior may break users who expect immediate vim takeover. Mitigate with explicit --external-editor/--with docs and possibly one release note.
PTY tests can be flaky if they depend on timing too strongly. Prefer fake command handshake markers where possible.
LM Studio responses are model-dependent. Cast proof markers should not require exact full answer text, only stable source/response presence markers.
Committed cast files can become stale. Metadata and validation tests must make staleness visible.
Never commit local LM Studio tokens or endpoint credentials.
dependencies: Existing operator TUI renderer and state models
Existing editor resolver and TUI tool registry
Existing website asset path for operator_tui_splash.cast
completion_definition: This todo is complete when the homepage uses a validated real PTY E2E TUI cast with Snake/Tutorial-AI/Artifacts proof markers and the default TUI file open path uses an inline read-only viewer instead of replacing the TUI process with an external editor.
